title: Nami Fights to Keep the Treasure
description: On pages 14–17, Nami’s obsession with treasure collides with Luffy’s blunt honesty and Buggy’s chaos.
scene: Nami tries to protect what she has stolen or earned, while the surrounding fight keeps pulling her deeper into the Straw Hats’ orbit.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: He will eat the devil's fruit!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Hahahah... being young is a wonder- full thing, since you can be so careful

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: Good going buggy. I have more respect for you now!!

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: So how is it buggy? Feel any differ- once in your body?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Well the story of the devil's fruit was a rumored story from the start

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: KEKEKEKEKEKEKEKEKEKEKEKEKE!! You've got it exactly right!! What I just ate was a fake fruit I spent all night working on and switching

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: EVERY- THING TURNED OUT AS PLANNED!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I don't need to be a pirate trainee anymore. I better get off this ship before anyone finds out

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: With the money from selling the fruit and the treasure from the map,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I could form the best pirate crew in the world and steal the most value-

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Your face sure is a sight

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: w...what the, so it was this idiot. Scared me half to death.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Oh yeah! If I think about it the captain just...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: you... you... you bastard, I... I....!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What is that? That piece of paper

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What the... my body can't move prop- e rely...

summary: The part keeps Nami morally complicated: she is selfish, clever, and frightened, but also increasingly tied to Luffy and Zoro.
